---
title: Quarto profiles
---

Profiles let a Quarto project reuse source documents while changing render
settings for a particular audience or product.

## Profile files

A base configuration might define shared formats and resources, while profile
files select output folders and filters:

```yaml
# _quarto-student.yml
project:
  output-dir: _output/student

filters:
  - filters/strip-solution.lua
```

```yaml
# _quarto-instructor.yml
project:
  output-dir: _output/instructor

filters:
  - filters/strip-exercise.lua
```

The project can then expose a task that passes the chosen profile to Quarto:

```bash
quarto render --profile student
```

Contributors generally run the equivalent `pixi run ...` task instead of
calling Quarto directly.

## Existing naming patterns

Different courses use `exercise`/`solution`, `student`/`instructor`, or
`student`/`master`. The labels are not interchangeable: use the configured
profile names and check the output paths associated with them.

## When profiles matter

Render all affected profiles when changing:

- Answer text or instructor guidance.
- Learner starter code or executable solution cells.
- Links between variants or shared navigation.
- Profile-dependent format output such as notebooks or PDFs.

Profile behavior may rely on filters or extensions as well as the YAML file,
so investigate all configured parts before changing the visibility model.
